hard.next_step: 10% confidence: May 13- no traction with account- BD to KIV. Apr 27 meeting with HoP last week; project still ongoing but no clear compelling event; still to clarify DC location requirements + local presence. Mar 13 KIV with BD. 08 Sep: request for eco estimation, internal budget approval, tender to be launched by Mar26, final decision expected by Sep26.
hard.ais_why: iContract (CLM) is the only product in scope with no AI SKUs, and six dated Next Step entries from Jul 2025 to Apr 2026 carry zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or GenAI. A focused single-module CLM evaluation with no AI dimension, gated by DC-location (Indonesia data residency) and a slow internal-budget approval cycle. Score 2 (AI Resistant).

north_star_verdict.headline: A validated but stalled CLM-only evaluation: M. Iqbal A.R. confirmed fit on the 3 Sep 2025 demo yet never returned the user counts and bilingual sample contracts needed to price, the Mar 2026 tender slipped to a next-year public tender, and no economic buyer has been mobilized for budget approval. A fresh owner (Carl Kimball, 1 Jul 2026) now has a chance to reset.
north_star_verdict.math: Re-qualified 24 Apr 2026, so ~73 days in the current Qualified stage; close date 16 Jul 2027 is 375 days out. Last CLM-substantive touch was the positive update call 1 Apr 2026; the only deep evaluation event was the demo 3 Sep 2025 (~10 months ago). Buyer replied to event invites through 12 Jun 2026 (declined Horizon 2026), but the initiative itself reads '10% confidence, no traction' (Next Step 13 May 2026). The Mar 2026 tender target passed with no launch; buyer reframed it as a next-year second-semester initiative. Long runway, but momentum is dead and no forcing function exists.
evidence: Buyer confirmed fit on the 3 Sep 2025 demo ('for all, it's fulfil our requirements', M. Iqbal A.R.) yet the deal has not progressed past Qualified in the 10 months since.
Two buyer deliverables owed since 3 Sep 2025 (power-user counts and Indonesian/bilingual sample contracts), both required before Zycus can price, remain undelivered.
Tender launch target of Mar 2026 missed; on the demo the buyer said the initiative would be 'next year, probably in the second semester'.
Next Step 13 May 2026: '10% confidence, no traction with account, BD to KIV'; no new date set.
Economic buyer CFO Indarto Pamungkas and Deputy CEO Nugroho Christijanto are named Evaluator Contact Roles but have never appeared on a call or activity; internal budget approval is the stated gate.
DC-location / Indonesia data-residency and PSRE/BSRE e-signature compliance flagged on the demo and in the Apr 2026 Next Step, still unresolved and likely tender-RFP blockers.
LastActivityDate 12 Jun 2026 is a Horizon 2026 event exchange the buyer politely declined, not CLM deal engagement.
Owner reassigned Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball on 1 Jul 2026, a fresh owner re-engaging a dormant state-owned-enterprise evaluation.

metrics.narrative: The business problem was articulated on the 3 Sep 2025 demo: manual contract drafting in Microsoft Word, multiple manual approval cycles, no standardized clause library, and fragmented collaboration causing delays and inconsistent clause usage, and M. Iqbal A.R. confirmed Zycus's read ('Yes, we are aligned with our understanding'). Zycus presented value targets of 80%-plus reduction in contract cycle time, 80%-plus reduction in sign-off approval time, and 90%-plus metadata-extraction accuracy. However, these are Zycus-presented benchmarks, not the buyer's own baseline: no current cycle time, manual-hours cost, off-contract leakage, or penalty exposure has ever been quantified in Pupuk's own numbers. Building the buyer's current-state cost case is what would turn a tolerable pain into a budget-justifying business case.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic-buyer path is named but never activated. CFO Indarto Pamungkas and Deputy CEO Nugroho Christijanto are Evaluator Contact Roles, and on the 3 Sep 2025 demo the buyer described the state-owned-enterprise process explicitly: 'we are preparing for the budget first. After the budget, we are going to have a tender' (M. Iqbal A.R.), confirming budget approval is the commercial gate. Yet neither the CFO nor the Deputy CEO has appeared on any call or activity, and pricing was left at a subscription-per-power-user model pending the buyer's user estimate. No C-level sponsor is engaged to force the budget or the tender, which is the single biggest reason the initiative keeps slipping. Reaching Indarto Pamungkas through M. Iqbal A.R. to validate the budget-approval path is the move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Indarto Pamungkas, CFO; Nugroho Christijanto, Deputy CEO)
Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025 (M. Iqbal A.R. on SOE budget-then-tender process)

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer surfaced concrete evaluation criteria on the 3 Sep 2025 demo: bilingual Indonesian-and-English contract handling ('for the international vendors, we are using two language in one contract... could you enhance that to Indonesian', M. Iqbal A.R.), modular deployment of CLM only while integrating with their existing procurement stack, SAP integration ('in Indonesia we use SAP... the integration with SAP', IT stakeholder), a defensible learning curve for Merlin AI, and a local Indonesian reference. The buyer also confirmed the formal criteria: the eventual tender will be judged on 'technical specifications, scope of work, and also competing in pricing'. Criteria are confirmed for the discovery phase but not yet formalized into an RFP requirements document, which the public tender will produce.
sources: Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025 (M. Iqbal A.R.; IT stakeholder)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer laid out the decision process on the 3 Sep 2025 demo and it is well understood: as a state-owned enterprise, Pupuk must first prepare an internal budget (using Zycus's pricing estimate), then run a mandatory public tender open to multiple vendors evaluated on technical fit and price, with the winner then contracted. M. Iqbal A.R. framed timing as 'the initiative will be next year, probably in the second semester'. The demo committee showed the buying group: procurement (M. Iqbal A.R., strategic evaluation), IT (integration and Merlin learning-curve questions), and legal (referenced as contract owners). The process is clear; the problem is that it has not started moving, the Mar 2026 tender target passed, and no one is driving budget approval.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting will run through a formal public tender governed by Indonesian SOE procurement rules, not a direct commercial close. Two compliance mechanics are already flagged: e-signatures must be PSRE/BSRE-certified per the Indonesian Ministry of IT ('if we want to use a digital signature, it should be authorized with PSRE/BSRE compliance', IT stakeholder, 3 Sep 2025), and DC-location / data-residency plus local-presence questions are unresolved (Next Step, 27 Apr 2026). The buyer currently signs offline. Pricing is subscription-based per power user (business users and vendors are free). MSA/SOW redlining, InfoSec, and legal channels have not been discussed because the deal has not reached commercials. Clearing the e-signature and data-residency questions before the RFP is what creates paper-process visibility.
sources: Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025 (IT stakeholder on PSRE/BSRE; sales director on subscription pricing)
SF Next_Step__c 27 Apr 2026 (DC location / local presence)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is a manual, Word-based contract lifecycle: the legal team drafts from scratch, standards vary by commodity ('every commodities like goods and services, they have their own contract standards', M. Iqbal A.R.), approvals cycle manually, and collaboration is fragmented, producing delays in supplier onboarding and project kickoff, heavy legal workload, and higher risk exposure. The pain owners are the procurement and legal functions represented by M. Iqbal A.R. (VP Strategic & Evaluation Procurement). The pain is confirmed and the buyer agreed with Zycus's read of it, but it is clearly tolerable: 10 months after a validating demo there is still no compelling event and no urgency, which is why the initiative keeps slipping. The unresolved gap is the cost of inaction, which has never been quantified.

champion.narrative: There is no functioning champion. M. Iqbal A.R. (VP Strategic & Evaluation Procurement, Contact Role: Decision Maker) is a cooperative evaluator: he led the 3 Sep 2025 demo, asked detailed questions, confirmed fit, committed to send user counts and sample contracts, and still responds to relationship touches (registered for the Apr 2026 Jakarta roundtable, positive call 1 Apr 2026, politely declined Horizon 2026 on 12 Jun 2026). But he has delivered neither committed artifact in 10 months, has not re-engaged the CLM initiative, has shown no access to the CFO or Deputy CEO, and has not driven the tender or budget internally. That is transactional cooperation, not advocacy. The other Contact Roles (Muhamad Iqbal Syaeful Hakim, Untung Suprayitno, Nugroho Christijanto, Indarto Pamungkas) have not appeared on any call, and partner WGS (Pingadi) has gone quiet since the demo. Building M. Iqbal A.R. into a real champion with economic-buyer access, or finding one, is the prerequisite to any progress.

competition.narrative: There is no active head-to-head bake-off today, but the buyer confirmed on 3 Sep 2025 that competition is coming: the award will run through a public tender open to 'other competitor also,' evaluated on technical fit and price. Coupa and GEP Worldwide are named in Salesforce Competitors__c and are the likely tender entrants, though no buyer preference for or evaluation of either has been stated. The real current competitor is do-nothing / status quo: the manual Word process is painful but tolerable, and 10 months of deferral show the buyer feels no urgency to launch the tender at all. Zycus's wedge is early-mover advantage: it is sole-sourced into discovery with a validated demo, plus Merlin clause-library AI, bilingual handling, SAP integration via iSaaS, and Certinal signing already positioned against the buyer's stated criteria. The risk is that a delayed tender lets Coupa and GEP enter on equal footing.
sources: Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025 (M. Iqbal A.R. on the tender being multi-vendor)

deal_movement.summary: Engagement is thinning: the only deep evaluation event remains the 3 Sep 2025 demo, followed by a positive CLM update call 1 Apr 2026. Since then the buyer has only exchanged event invitations (declining Horizon 2026 on 12 Jun 2026). Next Step reads '10% confidence, no traction'. The recent movement is internal Zycus housekeeping (owner change to Carl Kimball 1 Jul 2026, close date pushed to 16 Jul 2027), not buyer progress.

items.change: Owner reassigned Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball, a fresh owner on the account
Buyer politely declined the Horizon 2026 (SEA Edition) event invitation
Close date pushed from 11 Dec 2026 to 16 Jul 2027
Next Step flagged '10% confidence, no traction with account, BD to KIV'
Re-qualified from Initial Interest to Qualified
Positive outbound CLM update call with M. Iqbal A.R. (shared initiative updates)

competitive_position.summary: No live bake-off today; competition is a future public tender. Do-nothing / status quo is the strongest current threat (high): 10 months of tolerable manual-process pain with no forcing function is what is actually blocking the deal. Coupa and GEP Worldwide (both medium) are the anticipated tender entrants named in Salesforce with no buyer preference or evaluation yet; the SOE tender will bring them in when it launches, but Zycus holds early-mover advantage from a sole-sourced, validated demo. No incumbent CLM system is being replaced. Ranking by current threat: (1) do-nothing, (2) GEP Worldwide, (3) Coupa.

competitors.name: Do-nothing (status quo)
competitors.quote: Right now, we are just learning about the services... After the budget, we are going to have a tender... the initiative will be next year, probably in the second semester (M. Iqbal A.R.).
competitors.source: Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025; SF Next_Step__c 13 May 2026 ('no traction')
competitors.how_we_win: Manufacture urgency with a quantified cost-of-inaction case and a local Bintang/Heineken reference; give M. Iqbal A.R. an internal budget-justification narrative to force the tender launch.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Listed in Salesforce Competitors__c. No buyer statements or call evidence of active evaluation; the SOE tender will bring GEP into the shortlist when it launches.
competitors.how_we_win: Lean on early-mover advantage from the sole-sourced, validated demo ('fulfils our requirements', 3 Sep 2025); wedge on Merlin clause-library AI and bilingual Indonesian-English contract support as Phase 1 differentiators.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Listed in Salesforce Competitors__c. No buyer statements or call evidence of active evaluation; the SOE tender will bring Coupa into the shortlist when it launches.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Certinal signing as cost-effective versus Adobe Sign/DocuSign and pre-clear Indonesian PSRE/BSRE and data-residency compliance in the tender response to neutralize a global-brand rival on local-fit grounds.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit was validated on the 3 Sep 2025 demo, with two localization criteria still exposed that will decide the tender.

items.criterion: Modular CLM-only deployment integrating with the buyer's existing procurement stack
items.quote: Certainly. Yes... it is modular based, so you don't have to avail to all the modules to get the AI capabilities (Kritika, Zycus).
We have integrated with SAP... the timeline is two to three months only (Kritika, Zycus), against IT's question 'in Indonesia we use SAP... the integration with SAP'.
Could you enhance that to Indonesian and also Indonesian and English content clauses? (M. Iqbal A.R.) - Zycus said it is 'completely possible' but must validate with buyer's sample contracts.
If we want to use a digital signature, it should be authorized with PSRE / BSRE compliance in Indonesia (IT stakeholder). Zycus: 'we might go back and check if we have it.'
Do you have some customers in Indonesia? Maybe we can benchmark to them (IT stakeholder). Zycus offered multinational locals (Bintang/Heineken, Danone) but no direct Indonesian CLM reference.

items.area: Quantified business case / cost of inaction
items.quote: Zycus presented 80%-plus cycle-time reduction benchmarks, but the buyer never quantified its own current cost, cycle time, or leakage.
Mar 2026 tender target passed with no launch; buyer reframed to 'next year, probably in the second semester' with no committed date.
CFO Indarto Pamungkas and Deputy CEO Nugroho Christijanto are Contact Roles but have never joined a call or activity; budget authority unvalidated.

items.area: No new buyer call this sweep
items.quote: Only the 3 Sep 2025 demo is transcribed; no CLM-substantive buyer call since. Read rests on that demo plus the activity trail.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a stalled, single-threaded, early-stage SOE evaluation with a future public tender; the win levers are re-engagement, multi-threading to power, and pre-shaping the RFP on local fit.
flags: Convert M. Iqbal A.R. from cooperative evaluator to real champion: give him a budget-justification case and the Bintang/Heineken reference to carry internally.
Break single-thread: map and engage CFO Indarto Pamungkas and the Deputy CEO to survive a committee-driven public tender.
Pre-empt localization blockers in writing (bilingual contracts, DC-location data residency, PSRE/BSRE e-sign) to shape the RFP in Zycus's favour before it publishes.

(VP Strategic & Evaluation Procurement, Decision Maker Contact Role) confirmed fit on 3 Sep 2025 and still answers relationship touches, but has not delivered his two committed artifacts in 10 months, has no visible access to the CFO or Deputy CEO, and has not driven the budget case or tender internally. That is transactional cooperation, not advocacy. Partner WGS (Pingadi) led the demo but has gone quiet since Sep 2025.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 2/Negative (AI Resistant). Merlin clause-library, GenAI intake, risk-scoring and metadata-extraction landed well on the 3 Sep 2025 demo, but the scope is deliberately CLM-only with no AI SKUs and the buyer already runs its own procurement stack, so this is a genuinely AI-light deal, not an under-positioned one.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 2, AIS_Status Negative. The demo showed Merlin GenAI clause and intake features were well received, but iContract is the only product in scope with no AI SKUs and the buyer has its own S2P stack, so the AI-Resistant read is accurate to the scope rather than a positioning failure.

items.detail: Internal budget approval is the stated gate and no economic buyer (CFO Indarto Pamungkas / Deputy CEO Nugroho Christijanto) has been mobilized.
Tender slipped from Mar 2026 to a next-year second-semester initiative; no compelling event and '10% confidence, no traction'.
State-owned-enterprise procurement forces a mandatory multi-vendor public tender judged on technical fit and price, so a validated demo does not secure the award.
Localization requirements unresolved: bilingual Indonesian-English contracts to validate, DC-location / data-residency, and PSRE/BSRE e-signature certification.

confidence_signals.summary: Cooling: no CLM-substantive buyer call since the 3 Sep 2025 demo, '10% confidence, no traction' on the account, and the last buyer touch was a declined event invite (12 Jun 2026). Not fully dark (buyer still replies to relationship touches and a fresh owner just took over), but momentum is dead.

items.expected_effect: Resets the relationship with a fresh owner and re-opens a dormant evaluation before it goes fully cold.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer confirmed fit ('it's fulfil our requirements', M. Iqbal A.R., 3 Sep 2025) but explicitly framed the decision as a neutral public tender against 'other competitor also' and is 'just learning about the services' from multiple providers, with no Zycus-specific lean.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain (manual Word drafting, fragmented approvals, legal workload) confirmed by the buyer, but no ROI, cycle-time, or cost figure was ever quantified in Pupuk's own numbers; the 80%-plus figures are Zycus benchmarks.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A validated but stalled CLM-only evaluation: M. Iqbal A.R. confirmed fit on the 3 Sep 2025 demo but has withheld the user counts and bilingual sample contracts needed to price for 10 months, the Mar 2026 tender slipped to a next-year public tender, and no economic buyer is engaged, so early-mover advantage is our only real lead.

win_position.text: We are sole-sourced into a validated CLM demo, but the award runs through a mandatory public state-owned-enterprise tender open to other vendors, so early-mover advantage is our only lead, not a preference.

No economic buyer is engaged: CFO Indarto Pamungkas and Deputy CEO Nugroho Christijanto are named Evaluators but have never joined a call, and internal budget approval is the stated gate (Next Step, 8 Sep 2025).
Competition is a future tender rather than a live bake-off, so Coupa and GEP Worldwide enter on equal footing on technical fit and price once it launches (M. Iqbal A.R., 3 Sep 2025).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is effectively dead: no CLM-substantive buyer call since the 3 Sep 2025 demo, and Next Step 13 May 2026 reads '10% confidence, no traction with account, BD to KIV'.
The Mar 2026 tender target passed with no launch; the buyer reframed it on the demo as a next-year second-semester initiative.
The last buyer touch, 12 Jun 2026, was a polite decline of the Horizon 2026 event invite, not deal engagement.
A fresh owner, Carl Kimball, took the account on 1 Jul 2026, giving a clean opening to reset the relationship.

customer_commitment.text: Two buyer deliverables owed since 3 Sep 2025, the power-user counts and Indonesian/bilingual sample contracts (both needed before Zycus can price), remain undelivered 10 months on.
The buyer has set no date and named no compelling event; tolerable manual-Word pain keeps the initiative slipping with no forcing function.
The buyer did confirm fit and asked for a pricing estimate and a Bintang/Heineken reference call on 3 Sep 2025, showing genuine but low-urgency interest.

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing / status quo is the strongest current threat: 10 months of deferral show the buyer feels no urgency to launch the tender at all.
Indonesian data-residency (DC location / local presence) and PSRE/BSRE e-signature compliance are unresolved and are likely tender-RFP blockers.
Single-thread risk: M. Iqbal A.R. is the only engaged contact, drives nothing internally, and has no access to the CFO or Deputy CEO.

champion_strength.evidence: M. Iqbal A.R. cooperates but has withheld two committed artifacts for 10 months and has no economic-buyer access; transactional, not advocacy.

customer_preference.evidence: Fit confirmed but decision framed as a neutral multi-vendor public tender; no Zycus-specific lean.

momentum.evidence: No CLM-substantive buyer call since 3 Sep 2025; '10% confidence, no traction'; tender slipped to next year.
